question_text: The Moon's gravitational field strength is about 1.6 N/kg compared to Earth's 10 N/kg. A 60 kg astronaut travels to the Moon. What will happen to their mass and weight?

options.label: A
options.text: Their mass stays 60 kg; their weight decreases to 96 N.

options.label: B
options.text: Their mass decreases to 10 kg; their weight stays 600 N.

options.label: C
options.text: Both their mass and weight stay the same as on Earth.

options.label: D
options.text: Their mass increases; their weight decreases.

explanation: Mass never changes, so it stays 60 kg. Weight changes with gravity: W = m × g = 60 × 1.6 = 96 N on the Moon, compared to 600 N on Earth.
